title: simaba borneensis
intro: Simaba borneensis, also known as the Borneo horse chestnut tree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The bark and leaves of Simaba borneensis have been used to treat various health conditions, such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, providing health benefits like reduced inflammation and improved immune function. Herbal preparations of Simaba borneensis are typically made from the bark and leaves, which are often dried and used to make teas, tinctures, or infusions for medicinal purposes.
